items.title: China accused of trying to 'bankrupt' Port Pirie and Hobart metal smelters

children.content: The SA treasurer claims a foreign government is undermining the profitability of the struggling Port Pirie metal smelter.

articles.title: Locals fear for jobs as Port Pirie smelter's future in doubt

children.content: Locals say the possible closure of the Port Pirie smelter and the loss of the 900 jobs associated with it would have ongoing effects on the city and the wider Spencer Gulf region.

image.altText: The sign for Ellen Street in the foreground with a large smoke stack from the lead smelter in the background

articles.title: Smelter jobs at stake as government assistance runs dry

children.content: South Australia's premier says there is a "national imperative" to keep smelters running, as a $135 million assistance package for Nyrstar operations at Port Pirie and Hobart expires.

image.altText: An aerial view of the Nyrstar smelter in Port Pirie

items.title: Calls for change after soccer club told it needs men's team to compete

children.content: Millicent United's women's side is flying this season, but without a men's team the club has been told they are unable to compete.

image.altText: Two women standing on a soccer pitch, holding a soccer ball.

items.title: Polar blast and snow to follow record May heat over south-east Australia

children.content: Just weeks from winter, and south-east Australia is still enjoying summertime warmth.

image.altText: saftey beach in victoria

items.title: SA's history preservation organisation faces questions over future

children.content: The National Trust of South Australia admits it is cash-poor and will need to improve its finances, amid disagreements with its regionally-based volunteers.

items.title: 'We need all our numbers': Liberal rules out SA upper house presidency bid

children.content: Labor's path to getting legislation through the upper house may get slightly more difficult after Liberal MLC Dennis Hood — who was strongly linked to the upper house presidency role — said he would remain on the floor of parliament.

image.altText: An MP stands in a corridor of parliament.

items.title: Thousands travel to outback Agfair despite fuel costs

children.content: Fuel costs have not stopped thousands of people from descending upon Broken Hill for the biannual Agfair.

image.altText: A young woman smiling at the camera holding a sheep close to her body

items.title: Migrant delivery driver says assault claim was not taken seriously by SA Police

children.content: A migrant delivery driver who alleges he was assaulted while working in Adelaide's CBD says he was not taken seriously by SA Police, who told him the case had been closed before he had given a written statement.

image.altText: A man of Chinese heritage wears a jacket and stands next to a hedge

items.title: Police confirm identity of remains found in SA bushland

children.content: SA Police say remains found in bushland south of Adelaide a week ago have been identified as belonging to Jeff Mundy, who was last seen by his family five years ago.

items.title: The alternative classroom disengaged students are choosing to be in

children.content: Of the students who have attended the Alternative Suspension program, 67 per cent have received no further suspensions.

items.title: SA government needs certainty on LIV Golf by October, premier says

children.content: The SA Premier says the government is "monitoring" the situation regarding LIV Golf closely, and would need a guarantee of its future in Adelaide by October.

items.title: The algal bloom may be 'gone', but many are still grappling with the fall-out

children.content: While testing has shown a dramatic improvement in South Australia's algal bloom situation, industry and experts fear it won't be a quick recovery.

items.title: Remembering SA's pain and battles behind the Murray-Darling Basin Plan

children.content: The Murray-Darling Basin Plan is under review, but with submissions due to close today, we take a step back in time to remember when dried lake beds were turning acidic, dairy farmers were selling up, and there was talk of water carting for Adelaide.

image.altText: Close up of cracks in dried lakebed

items.title: 'Children have ended up on main roads', SA childcare regulator says

children.content: South Australia’s childcare centre watchdog says children have injured themselves or "ended up on main roads" due to poor supervision and that one in five services in the state do not meet national quality standards.

image.altText: A man in a dark blue suit and tie is sitting and looking ahead

items.title: Australian businesses falling victim to new type of fraud

children.content: Higher inflation, a war in the Middle East, and a ban on surcharges are crippling small businesses. Now they are falling victim to a modern crime.

items.title: Russian woman's husband beat her but police were no help, so she wrote to Putin

children.content: Domestic violence and abuse have been described as an "epidemic" in multiple countries, but analysts argue there is something that sets Russia apart: the government is making things worse.

items.title: Iran ready for 'renewed' hostilities as Trump cools on peace proposal

items.title: Police charge Jefferson Lewis, 47, with 5yo girl's murder

image.altText: A mugshot of an Aboriginal man, serious expression looking directly at camera, yellow / orange pigment behind brown eyes.

children.content: Northern Territory Police have charged Jefferson Lewis with the murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby in Alice Springs.

items.title: Contaminants are in your clothes but brands don't need to tell you

children.content: Despite the rising flood of fast fashion coming into Australia, there's little regulation on hazardous substances in clothes.

image.altText: Dozens of brightly coloured T-shirts hanging off coathangers in a clothing store.

items.title: Housing provider blamed tenant for soaked carpet, then regulator stepped in

children.content: Despite unsafe and unusable bathrooms, cockroach infestations eating through wiring and constantly damp carpets, some Tasmanian public housing tenants are having to get the regulator to intervene in order to get solutions.

items.title: What can you use your personal leave for?

children.content: People employed on a permanent basis in Australia are entitled to 10 days' paid personal leave a year. So what are the different types of personal leave and when can you use them?

items.title: As fuel costs soar, cycling advocates argue our priorities are all wrong

items.title: No to high-rise in our suburbs, new Victorian campaign group argues

children.content: Residents' groups across Victoria have formed an alliance ahead of the state election, calling for the community and councils to be given a voice when it comes to approving high-density housing.

items.title: Man accused of murdering parents and brother in 'grisly' attack

items.title: What you need to know about Queensland's train station staffing changes

children.content: Queensland Rail says it is moving to a system where staffing is based on "customer needs and patronage", but the changes have been labelled as "extreme" that will make some stations less safe.
